summary: Add support for sending audio sources to multiple buses

description.text:
This PR implements a `sends` parameter on `AudioStreamPlayer`, `AudioStreamPlayer2D`, `AudioStreamPlayer3D`, and `Area2D`, allowing these types to send their audio stream to more than one bus simultaneously.

Resolves https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/1836.
Supersedes #60316.

### Design

The parameter itself is a `Dictionary[StringName, float]`, where the key is the name of the bus, and the value is the send's gain.
This allows different buses to receive a node's stream at varying volume levels.

It is presented using a normal `Dictionary` inspector property, with its hint string configured to show an option picker for the name and a range slider for the gain.

The existing `bus` property keeps its purpose as the primary bus: while the `sends` dictionary can be empty, `bus` must be specified or it will default to "Master" (even if there are sends present).

All sends respect their node's panning and gain.

Sends are not overridden when an `AudioStreamPlayer2D`/`3D` enters an `Area2D`/`3D` with `audio_bus_override` enabled; in these cases, only the bus specified by `bus` will be overridden.

### Limitations

The most notable limitation is that sample playback is not supported in this current implementation. If using sample playback, audio will only be sent to the primary bus as specified by `bus`.

Additionally, sends on `Area3D` have not been implemented due to complexity with the already-existing reverb send parameter.

These might be good to follow up with in the future, although I don't believe they are central enough to preclude this feature.

### Compatibility

All preexisting outward API is unmodified, and care has been taken to preserve existing behavior, so this should not break compatibility.

summary: Add support for embedding subwindows and dual screen GameViews

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11091

Thanks to guidance from @Hilderin this PR adds two new features to the Game tab and embedding tools that were introduced in Godot 4.4. The first change extends the embedding functionality to allow embedding of "subwindows" (as they're known in `DisplayServer`) when provided with a unique window name. The user can now select whether to embed the main window or a custom window that matches the name provided via a text box in the GameView:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3abf1bf9-cbf6-422e-9cfa-a80aef3a6843

This allows developers of multi-window games to embed the game window that makes the most sense for their project rather than being stuck with the main window. This is accomplished through modifications to the display server so that a subwindow can be parented much the same as the main window. In order to send the parenting data to the built game, a new `--swid` (subwindow ID) command line parameter was created that matches the `--wid` implementation for the main window. Then, when the `GameView` modifies the instance parameters, it can optionally send the `--swid` with the user provided window name.

As suggested by @Hilderin , I took the next step in the proposal to better utilize subwindow embedding here by adding an option to split the Game tab into two GameViews for better visibility while debugging. This was largely done by splitting out functionality that used to be handled by the `GameView` into the `GameViewPlugin` and having the plugin manage two instances, but all together it works pretty cleanly! A button in the embed options popup lets you split the view and the layout is saved as project metadata, with the default setting being the regular single view that people are familiar with:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/462f88d1-00c5-409b-9334-e6716db84516

Since this PR was already looking like a lot to review, one step I left for a future PR was getting the secondary view's game debugger working properly. This proved challenging to do immediately because we would either need to make the RuntimeSelect stuff not a singleton or we'd need to pass window data through every message sent via the debugger, but I'm certain that one of these solutions will be easier to work out when this initial PR is wrapped up. Besides that, the regular debugger controls for the main GameView are still fully functional:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d6c88390-028c-46e4-86bf-3a5d54ccdbd0

**Note:** This PR only implements subwindow embedding for the Windows display server. Sadly I don't have a Linux machine to test on but Hilderin said it was fine to leave it like this so that a follow up PR can tackle the Linux/Android support which I believe shouldn't be too tough to match from Windows.

Thank you to everyone who offered ideas about the future of embedding in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/99010 and I hope this new functionality proves to be helpful for developers looking for more debugging options! Let me know if there's anything I've missed and I'll be happy to make adjustments :)

description.text:

summary: [DRAFT] Allow android/xr editor to begin gradle builds

description.text:
*Edit 2025-04-23: Adding new setup instructions / comments to the top of PR description*

This PR is a proof of concept at this point, that with some minimal setup Godot can have termux install all the needed dependencies and then build an APK.  **Note: this process currently automatically accepts android SDK license agreements**, this is temporary and just for ease of testing.

Here's some info on how to set it up if you'd like to give it a try, do note that on first run the build will take quite a while, at least five or six minutes for me on Quest 3. After an initial successful build, another build takes maybe around a minute.

### Setup
- Download the latest [Termux arm64 apk](https://github.com/termux/termux-app/releases/tag/v0.118.2) and install on your device (I've only tested this on Meta Quest 3)
- Open up termux and run the command `termux-setup-storage`
- Edit the `~/.termux/termux.properties` file and uncomment the line `allow-external-apps = true`
- Grant the com.termux.permission.RUN_COMMAND permission to Godot with adb:
```
adb shell pm grant org.godotengine.editor.v4.meta.debug com.termux.permission.RUN_COMMAND
```
- Install the android build templates built from this branch, or you can just use the 4.4-stable build template and replace the `build.gradle` file by hand

After that, it _should_ be able to execute a build, but I will not be surprised at all if I've missed something and people bump into issues.

### Current Termux Command Flow on Build
- Check if OpenJDK 17 is installed
  - If not, install it with `pkg`
- Check if all android sdk packages are installed with `sdkmanager`
  - If this fails, it will run a gradle task to install `wget`, `unzip`, `sdkmanager`, and all the other android sdk stuff.
- Check if aapt2 is installed
  - If not, install it with `pkg`
- Run a gradle build with an extra task added to find and replace any aapt2 files found in `~/.gradle`
  - If it fails (and it will fail the first time ever attempted) it will attempt once more. This is just a hacky workaround for the time being. After that initial fail, aapt2 will be swapped and it shouldn't fail again.
- Copy and rename the built file with gradle

---
*Edit 2025-04-23: Outdated initial PR description*

At the moment this PR is just for testing purposes. These changes allow android devices to install android build templates and then begin the gradle build process, staging the necessary files for a gradle build, but will exit before actually attempting the gradle build command.

I've only tested with this patched to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/4c311cbee68c0b66ff8ebb8b0defdd9979dd2a41 on the XR Editor.

summary: Improve default/no query quick open dialog behavior

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/99672
Close #103558

This change primarily removes `SHOW_ALL_FILES_THRESHOLD` and was motivated by someone thinking quick open had a regression after having saved 31 instances of some resource. The two default/empty query behaviors (show all, show history) are combined such that up to `filesystem/quick_open_dialog/max_results` results are shown, prioritizing items in history and matching the tie-break ordering used by fuzzy search.

Separately, this fixes a minor bug where elements in history try to get a resource preview using their uid string and end up displaying the default icon. The cache file still saves by uid rather than by path.

Performance wise (on a project with ~700 resource files) this adds 1ms to the initialization time for the popup but saves 3ms while updating the results (for both popup and query changes). Both versions have a 360ms first launch time and 100ms display mode switch time; maybe those could be improved in the future.

### Issue description

When writing a custom resource format loader `MyFmtLdr` that inherits `ResourceFormatLoader`, every project rebuild will cause the Godot Editor to print

```csharp
[GlobalClass]
public partial class MyFmtLdr : ResourceFormatLoader
{

    public override bool _HandlesType(StringName type) => true;
    public override string _GetResourceType(string path) => nameof(MyFormatResource);
    public override Variant _Load(string path, string originalPath, bool useSubThreads, int cacheMode) =>
        new MyFormatResource { Data = FileAccess.GetFileAsString(path) };
}
```

`MyFormatResource.cs`
```csharp
[GlobalClass]
public partial class MyFormatResource : Resource
{
    [Export] public string Data { get; set; }

    public override void _ValidateProperty(Dictionary property)
    {
        if (property.TryGetValue("name", out var value)
            && value.AsStringName() == PropertyName.Data
            && property.ContainsKey("usage"))
            property["usage"] = Variant.From(PropertyUsageFlags.Default | PropertyUsageFlags.ScriptVariable | PropertyUsageFlags.ReadOnly);
    }
}
```

```
Lorem Ipsum is simply dummy text of the printing and typesetting industry.
```

summary: Animation Playback Track has a single frame delay

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.3.stable and 4.4.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable.mono unknown - Arch Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Fri, 07 Mar 2025 20:19:00 +0000 on Wayland - Wayland display driver, Single-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Ti (nvidia; 570.124.04) - Intel(R) Xeon(R) CPU E3-1231 v3 @ 3.40GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

Animations started from `AnimationPlayer` with an _Animation Playback Track_ seem to have a single frame (?) delay before the new animation applies the first frame keys. This is observable if the same `AnimationPlayer` simultaneously applies changes to a node via an _Animation Playback Track_ and a _Property Track_. This causes e.g. a sprite to flash at an incorrect position for a fraction of a second, if `offset` of a sprite is modified at the same keyframe as animation changes.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3bf477e9-fd48-43e5-b34f-e9357e9c97da)

I have a scene set up as visible in the screenshot. A `AnimatedSprite2D` is driven by a `AnimationPlayer` (`CharacterAnimations`) to play character animations. This character is used as part of a larger scene, where another `AnimationPlayer` (`CutsceneAnimations`) orchestrates playing out a cutscene. The `CutsceneAnimations` controls `CharacterAnimations` via an _Animation Playback Track_, but needs to apply some of its own adjustments to the player character via additional _Property Tracks_.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f9aef9b-e273-45c8-b55d-d664a69a6d1f)

At 1.8 seconds in the animation, `CutsceneAnimations` updates the currently playing animation on `CharacterAnimations` and simultaneously adjusts `AnimatedSprite2D:offset` to make the pixels in the last frame of the previous animation and the first frame of the next animation match. At this point, before and after the frame, it is expected that player character is positioned like this:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dee33168-1d50-41a9-9ced-90b08996d957)

However, when the animation is played, for a fraction of a second, presumably a single frame, the player character can be observed in an unexpected position:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ff98486b-931c-4149-9945-4b9682de9035)

The sprite here is still displaying the last frame of the first animation, with the offset set at 1.8 seconds. At the next frame, the sprite updates to the first key of the second animation, updating the `animation` and `frame` properties, thus positioning the character correctly.

The issue does not seem to reproduce when seeking to 1.8 seconds in the animation editor panel.

Here, I would expect that an _Animation Playback Track_ and a _Property Track_ applying adjustments on the exact same keyframe would be observable at the same rendered frame. Currently, there seems to be a single frame delay.

#### Workarounds

Issue can be slightly mitigated by adjusting the `AnimatedSprite2D:offset` at a very slight delay (0.1-0.01 seconds), which is likely to delay the property track enough to perform the adjustment on the same rendered frame as the animation playback track. This isn't 100% effective as the solution is framerate dependent (the update might still occasionally happen on different frame).

An effective workaround seems to be to reset the `AnimatedSprite2D:animation` and `:frame` with their own _Property Tracks_, like this:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/402a7a38-89b4-4142-9780-1f3101bd88dd)

The added property tracks reset the sprite to the exact same state as `idle_side` animation does at the first frame. As the _Property Tracks_ all get applied at the same rendered frame, the changes are immediately observable, and no flashing occurs. The _Animation Playback Track_ takes over at the next rendered frame, and everything is fine in the world.

While the workaround effectively mitigates the issue, it is unnecessarily cumbersome and error-prone, especially if multiple _Property Tracks_ and multiple _Animation Playback Tracks_ are involved.

### Steps to reproduce

The example repro uses an `AnimatedSprite2D` with sprite animations and offsets to show a simplified version of where I first encountered this. However basically any property works for the repro. For a minimal repro, e.g. `Sprite2D:position` can be animated as follows:
1. Set up a scene with two `AnimationPlayer`s (`A` and `B`) and a `Sprite2D` (`S`)
2. Create a new animation for `A` with _Property Track_ for `S:position`.  Set the position track as _Discrete_.
3. Create a new animation for `B` with _Animation Playback Track_ for `A` and a _Property Track_ for `S:position`. Set the position track as _Discrete_.
4. On `A`'s animation, add keyframe for `S:position` at 0s with position at `(0,0)`
5. On `B`'s animation, add keyframe for `S:position` at 0s with position at `(0,0)` and `(32,0)` at 0.5s
6. On `B`'s animation, add keyframe to play `A`'s animation at 0.5s
7. Now, playing the `B`'s animation, `S` can be seen _very_ briefly flashing at `(32,0)`, at exactly 0.5 seconds, and shifting back to `(0,0)` immediately afterwards.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[AnimationTrackFrameDelayRepro.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19215448/AnimationTrackFrameDelayRepro.zip)

summary: AudioEffectPitchShift cancels out other AudioEffects

description.text:
### Tested versions

Issue is found in Godot 4.4
Tested in Godot 4.3 and bug cannot be reproduced









### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - AMD Ryzen 7 7435HS (16 threads)

### Issue description

Playing an audio file from AudioStreamPlayer2D. 

when using pitch shift effect in any bus channel, if any other effects are added then pitch shift cancels out the other effects. If they are on the same bus then there is no audio. If effects are separate i.e pitch shift on master bus and other effects on other buses then audio is heard but other effects are cancelled out and their "effect" is not heard.




### Steps to reproduce

Playing an audio file from AudioStreamPlayer2D. 

Using AudioEffectPitchShift on the master bus - works ok
Add another effect to the master bus - no audio when play()
Uncheck AudioEffectPitchShift in master bus - audio ok when play()

Create bus channel, route AudioStreamPlayer2D to new bus,
move other effect to new master bus, 
AudioEffectPitchShift remains in master bus - audio is now heard but other effects are cancelled out by AudioEffectPitchShift
Uncheck AudioEffectPitchShift in master bus - new bus effects work again

summary: GDScript debugger not properly freeing memory when project closed

description.text:
### Tested versions

 - Reproducable in v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee], 4.3.stable.official

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6614) - AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

I am currently developing a sort of fantasy console, and am using the Godot game engine for both the console code editor, debugger, and emulator.
As a fantasy console, the emulator must use its own memory, so I have an Array of integers to represent the memory at all times (the memory is fixed size with direct addressing in the vein of older consoles like the NES, SNES, and Gameboy.

While I was developing the emulator, I noticed that Godot was using a rather insane amount of memory, around 12GB. I at first assumed this was down to my poor programming abilities and that the memory array had bloated to an unreal size. But when I closed the program (not the editor), the memory stayed bloated, meaning it was the editor and not my program.

After some experimentation, I realized that it was because of a breakpoint I had set up to make sure the memory array was initialized correctly. Every time it got tripped the memory Godot was using increased, without properly decreasing. In addition, if the breakpoint was not there, the memory would remain unchanging, either bloated or normal.

In addition, the amount of arrays matters, as the more arrays you have the faster memory will get consumed. Because of this, I think the issue is down to the stack variables display, as a much larger array will take less memory than multiple smaller ones.

I uploaded a video demonstrating the issue I had. It is at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LNIh0Hru7s8 (the file wouldn't upload to GitHub)

I only tested v4.3 and v4.4 as those were the only ones I had on me at the time, so I'm not sure when this issue came up version-wise.

I only noticed this because of my memory-hogging memory arrays, but in a smaller game that uses less memory, I'm sure this issue would go under the radar.

### Steps to reproduce

To reproduce, create an extremely large array with the resize() function and then use a for loop to populate it with something. (My arrays are populated with zeros instead of nulls, so you can do that as well for maximum accuracy). Make a breakpoint after the array is created and watch as the memory increases. Starting the project multiple times or navigating the stack frames causes memory usage to increase.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19204536/bug.zip)

summary: Improve editor 2D/3D main screen auto-switching logic

description.text:
In master, Godot has a feature where the main screen tabs may auto-switch depending on the node type being edited.

* When editing a 2D scene or selecting a 2D node, and the 3D tab is selected, switch to 2D.
* When editing a 3D scene or selecting a 3D node, and the 2D tab is selected, switch to 3D.
* When creating a new 2D or 3D scene, and the tab isn't the script editor (2D/3D/Game/AssetLib/custom), switch.

Seems simple enough. However, the code currently has the following problems:

* There is a hard-coded assumption that the script editor is at index 2.
* Instead of using `func _handles` on each plugin, there is hard-coded logic to check for CanvasItem and Node3D.
* The logic does not behave the same when creating scenes as it does when editing scenes or nodes.
* There is some dead code in the script editor for deciding when to switch (it always returns true, but never happens).

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4661d7e1-34de-41e3-b18c-b4e21813f897

This PR makes the following changes:

* New function `bool EditorMainScreen::can_auto_switch_screens() const`.
    * This is used both when editing nodes/scenes and when creating new ones, so now the logic is unified.
    * Instead of hard-coding an index for Script, check what position it's at. The new logic is that any tab to the left of Script is considered auto-switch-away-able, and auto-switch-to-able is determined by `func _handles`.
    * For the built-in tabs, this does change behavior in one case: creating a new scene when either the Game or AssetLib tabs are active will keep you on those tabs, just like editing a scene already does.
* Set a human-readable unique name for the EditorMainScreenButtons HBoxContainer.
* Remove the dead `can_take_away_focus` and `can_lose_focus_on_node_selection` functions.

This now works correctly for custom node types and main screen tabs placed to the left of Script:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bfb16b61-b4a3-466c-8955-ac2319dbb1cf

Implements and closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12154

summary: get_global_mouse_position() returns the wrong value after switching to a mirrored display while in fullscreen, on MacOS

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee], v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8].

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.2.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M3 Max - Apple M3 Max (16 threads)

### Issue description

On my MacBook Pro M3 Max, when I plug in an external, mirrored display (in my case LG TV via HDMI), the result of `get_global_mouse_position()` begins to return an incorrect value, which is consistently offset from the correct value.

Here's a short video demonstrating the issue: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZbdPsmmn6lA

The issue is most easily demonstrated with this code, that updates a sprite's global position to the cursor position every frame in `_process`:

```
sprite_2d.global_position = get_global_mouse_position()
```

Here's a screenshot showing the result when the bug is active, demonstrating that `get_global_mouse_position()` is returning something other than the cursor position:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c402aad8-36cf-4ea2-abea-8a38eb6c2205)

It is expected to look like this, with the sprite centered on the cursor:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/978d275f-af1e-4b7b-a18a-e313fbf56684)

After a lot of reading of [window.cpp](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/scene/main/window.cpp) code, and checking transforms, I determined that the only difference before and after plugging in the HDMI cable was in `Window.position`, which [modifies the screen transform like this](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/scene/main/window.cpp#L2817).

As I said, I tracked down the issue to an incorrect value of `Window.position` after plugging in an external display that mirrors the screen.  I saw that in [`window.cpp`, position is set using `DisplayServer::get_singleton()->window_get_position`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/0a30831bed69bcf5f7f20f736e23879052b1e5ab/scene/main/window.cpp#L1394). With this information, I was able to craft a workaround that seems to work reasonably well if it's run on every frame in `_process`:

```
var window = get_viewport() as Window
window.position = DisplayServer.window_get_position(window.get_window_id())
```

The fact that this workaround works shows (I think) that the issue lies in `Window.position` either missing being updated somewhere, or incorrectly being updated.

### Steps to reproduce

Example reproduction using MRP: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZbdPsmmn6lA

1. Set up an external display as a mirror of the "Entire Screen". In my case, I'm mirroring to my LG TV via HDMI: ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9085df3e-ca0b-487d-b767-4f36ce77762a)

2. Unplug the external display.

3. Start up the MRP. Fullscreen it.

5. You will see a button that says "FIX IS ON" or "FIX IS OFF". Switch it to "FIX IS OFF" to reproduce the bug.

6. Before doing anything else, move the cursor around and observe that the sprite follows the cursor exactly. It does so by setting the sprite's global position to the result of `get_global_mouse_position()`.

7. Now, plug in the external display. The screen will jump a couple of times.

8. Observe that the sprite is now offset above the cursor. It tracks at an offset:

    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c402aad8-36cf-4ea2-abea-8a38eb6c2205)

9. Switch to another window and back, and the issue resolves itself and the cursor now tracks correctly again.

10. Unplug the external display, and try the same steps with "FIX IS ON", which implements the workaround explained in the issue description. Now when you plug in the external display, the sprite continues to track the cursor correctly.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

https://github.com/jasharpe/godot-window-position

[window-position-repro.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19202967/window-position-repro.zip)

summary: Worker threads block the main thread if started from Area2D.body_entered/RigidBody2D.body_entered on Web

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.3.dev3.official [36e943b6b], v4.3.dev4.official [df78c0636], v4.3.beta2.official [b75f0485b], v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8], v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]
- Not reproducible in: v4.2.2.stable.official [15073afe3], v4.3.dev1.official [9d1cbab1c], v4.3.dev2.official [352434668]
- Likely introduced in v4.3.dev3.official [36e943b6b]

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon(TM) Graphics (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 31.0.21916.1002) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800H with Radeon Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

When a `RigidBody2D.body_entered` or `Area2D.body_entered` signal calls a function that contains a call to `WorkerThreadPool.start_task`, the worker thread will seemingly block the main thread until the task is completed. This only appears to happen when the game is exported to Web.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Have a `RigidBody2D` or `Area2D`, and connect the `body_entered` signal to the script
2. Have the function that is called by the signal start a worker thread task
3. Export the game to Web, start the game on Web, and try to trigger the `body_entered` signal
4. Observe that the game freezes when you do that

Example code:
```gdscript
extends Node

func _on_area_2d_body_entered(body):
	WorkerThreadPool.add_task(do_nothing_for_5_seconds)

func do_nothing_for_5_seconds():
	var t = Time.get_ticks_msec()
	while Time.get_ticks_msec() - t < 5000:
		pass
```

Also note that this still happens even if starting the worker thread task is in a deferred call, like so:
```gdscript
extends Node

func _on_area_2d_body_entered(body):
	call_deferred("start_task")

func start_task():
	WorkerThreadPool.add_task(do_nothing_for_5_seconds)

func do_nothing_for_5_seconds():
	var t = Time.get_ticks_msec()
	while Time.get_ticks_msec() - t < 5000:
		pass
```

**Instructions on how to use the MRP are included in the project itself as a help window.**

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[Download mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19199960/mrp.zip)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8cfe9d6d-85c9-4228-b233-9f6ccb2bec44)

Things to note:
- Whenever I move the green logo over the red logo, it freezes the game because a worker thread task was started when `Area2D.body_entered` emitted, and that worker thread (whose task lasts 5 seconds) ends up blocking the main thread for no reason.
- Whenever I move my mouse over the cyan logo, it also starts a worker thread task that lasts for 5 seconds, but it does not block the main thread because it was initiated by `CollisionBody2D.mouse_entered` rather than `Area2D.body_entered`/`RigidBody2D.body_entered`.

### System information

### Issue description

When specifying `@export_range` for a float value, Godot suggested I put one of the specialized string options (`"or_greater"`, etc.) on 3rd argument. I thought that was odd, since _I know_ there is supposed to be a `step` as a third argument. So I placed my _step_ in front of the suggested string and it really errored!

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a1c5ec19-feb4-4086-acc6-b095641b72d5)

I think this is just the editor's wrong suggestion. Docs are clear on this, extra hints should come from 4th place onward.

### Steps to reproduce

- Start typing `@export_range(0.0, 10.0, `
- Wait for completion to trigger
- String option will be suggested, which it shouldn't be, as this is the place for `step` parameter, not `extra_hints`

summary: Clear Editor Plugins for Folded Inspector Sub-Properties.

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/103976

Possibly addresses this issue https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/103615, but more testing is required.

This PR fixes a minor bug where the EditorPropertyResources which had corresponding editor plugins associated with a descendent properties, would not cleanup editor plugins from the list if the ancestor property was folded. It does this now by simply walking the sub-inspector and recursively checking the property maps beneath it until it finds another EditorPropertyResource with the 'opened_editor' flag set. If one is found, it will perform the cleanup hide_unused_editors method. An example of this error occurring is when opening a Shader resource inside a ShaderMaterial resource assigned to a MeshInstance, then selecting and deselecting the ShaderMaterial property in the inspector.

summary: Editor Plugins not removed when Folding an ancestor Resource

description.text:
### Tested versions

78c9f8ddd906273a5970518d54d2f5114d07b246

### System information

Windows 11

### Issue description

Possibly related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/103615, but I have been unable to reproduce the crash to know for sure.

This is a minor bug in the inspector a resource property, which had corresponding editor plugins associated with a descendent properties, does not cleanup editor plugins from the list if the ancestor property was folded. This is because the code associated with removing a plugin from the active list is only called if the exact property with an associated plugin which handles the type is deselected. If, however, an EditorPropertyResource which contains a property handled by an plugin is closed while the property handled by a plugin is open, it will not be removed from the active list and the editor will show a warning that it tried to add the plugin to the list twice while it is already open.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3ca0cc80-f370-4d84-a65c-5c72d8791c12

### Steps to reproduce

* Create a MeshInstance.
* Assign it a ShaderMaterial.
* Assign the ShaderMaterial a Shader.
* Open the Shader property as shown in the video.
* Click on the ShaderMaterial property. to close it.
* Click on it again to reopen it and note the error.

summary: Clear Orphaned Node Connections.

description.text:
Fully closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/101854

While we just merged a PR which will prevent a crash, this should provide the correct way of handling orphaned signals when casually deleting nodes via the SceneTreeDock. Ensures that node connections are properly disconnected/reconnected when deleting nodes via UndoRedo. Orphaned signal connections are properly removed upon saving a scene, so removing them immediately should be fine and will prevent cryptic errors from popping up.
